Mathematics Lesson
Topic: Quadratic Equations
Class: SSS2

Today we will learn about quadratic equations. A quadratic equation is ax²+bx+c=0.

John and Mary will come to the board to try some examples.
Peter, please solve x²-5x+6=0.
The teacher asks James to factorise x²+7x+12.

Solution: x²-5x+6 = (x-2)(x-3), so x=2 or x=3

For homework, William and Elizabeth should solve the following five equations from the textbook.
Remember that Michael showed us this method last week.

Quiz: Solve x²-x-12=0 and verify by substitution.
